quotes.text: My grandmother could end a whole argument with one look — didn't say shit, just turned her head real slow, like an owl that pays taxes, and grown men suddenly remembered they had somewhere to be. I've met men who ran whole city blocks with less power than that woman had in a single eyebrow.
quotes.speaker: Richard Pryor
quotes.context: On growing up, from a 1979 concert-film stage set
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the family-storytelling warmth with a sudden vivid image ('an owl that pays taxes') is pure Pryor. The tell: his real concert bits built to a bigger, riskier payoff; this one stays cozy where the real one would've gone for the throat.

quotes.text: My sex life is so bad, my G-spot has been declared a historical landmark.
quotes.speaker: Joan Rivers
quotes.context: Rivers being characteristically filthy and self-deprecating about her own dried-up sex life.

quotes.text: You've baked a really lovely cake, but then you've used dog shit for frosting.
quotes.speaker: Steve Jobs
quotes.context: Critiquing a NeXT programmer's work during a project review

source.title: Book 'The Second Coming of Steve Jobs' (Cult of Mac)

quotes.text: The record business is a room full of accountants in expensive sneakers trying to sell you a feeling they have personally never had. They'll slap a warning label on a guitar solo before they'd ever put one on the contract — and the contract is the only genuinely obscene thing in the building.
quotes.speaker: Frank Zappa
quotes.context: On the music industry, a 1986 television interview
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Zappa's contempt for the industry and the warning-label jab (his real crusade) are exactly his soapbox. The tell: it's too tidy — the real Zappa would've named the accountants, the label, and the specific clause.

quotes.text: People say you can't put a price on a good night's sleep, and I say you absolutely can — it's the price of the mattress, I've done the math, and frankly some of you are wildly overpaying to feel rested. That's not wisdom, friend. That's just a receipt.
quotes.speaker: Norm Macdonald
quotes.context: A deadpan run during a 2011 appearance on Conan
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the flat, over-literal dismantling of a cliché, capped with 'that's just a receipt,' is the Norm Macdonald special. The tell: a real Norm bit would've taken a sharp left into something absurd and slightly menacing by now.

quotes.text: People ask if roasting is mean. Mean? I'm doing these poor bastards a FAVOR — for one night, every awful thing they already know about themselves gets said out loud, by a professional, with perfect timing. That's not cruelty. That's closure with better lighting.
quotes.speaker: Greg Giraldo
quotes.context: On roast comedy, a 2009 Comedy Central Roast green-room interview
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the lawyerly logic defending roast cruelty as a public service is exactly Giraldo's angle. The tell: the real Giraldo would've aimed it at a specific roastee in the room, not 'these poor bastards' in general.
